task.prompt: Write a function `price_after_rules(amount_cents, rules)` that applies price adjustment rules to an amount.

gold_spec.goal: Apply a comma-separated rule string to an integer price in cents with exact money rounding.
postconditions: Split rules on commas and trim ASCII whitespace around each chunk.
Supported chunks are exactly lowercase 'N% off', '$D.CC off', '$D.CC fee', and 'tax N%'.
N may be an integer or decimal percent with at most two digits after the decimal point.
Ignore malformed chunks and case-mismatched keywords.
Apply all off rules first in their original order, then all fee rules, then all tax rules.
For percent off, subtract floor(current_cents * percent / 100).
For fixed off, subtract the parsed cents amount.
Clamp the running amount to zero after every off rule.
For fee, add the parsed cents amount.
For tax, add tax rounded half up to the nearest cent.
edge_cases: Repeated percent discounts are sequential, not combined.
Tax is applied after discounts and fees even if its text appears earlier.
A fixed discount larger than the current amount clamps to zero before fees.
acceptance_criteria: The function returns an integer cent amount using only integer arithmetic.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt hides exact rule grammar, case sensitivity, discount ordering, tax ordering, clamping, and floor versus half-up rounding.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: missing integer rounding and adjustment precedence semantics
